frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RPE account broadened to include sensory prediction errors beyond reward.

frameworks.name: reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: Canonical dopamine-as-RPE hypothesis taken as the starting point.

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Knowledge of sensory structure invoked to explain non-reward dopamine signals.

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Pure cached-value account argued to be insufficient for new findings.

core_question: Can dopamine signals be reconceived as a generalized prediction error covering both sensory and reward predictions?
key_assumptions: Dopamine transients reflect prediction errors of some kind
RL provides the right computational vocabulary for dopamine function
Sensory and reward predictions can share a common error signal
manipulations: Theoretical broadening of the prediction-error definition (sensory vs reward)
Algorithmic positioning between model-based and model-free RL
key_findings: Existing evidence shows dopamine activity also tracks sensory prediction errors unrelated to reward
A generalized-PE account remains consistent with classic dopamine-RPE correspondence
The account explains sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking
interpretations: Dopamine supports a hybrid RL regime sitting between model-based and model-free algorithms
Treating dopamine as a generalized PE unifies reward and non-reward phenomena under one signal
